source_alignment.subject: Mathematics
source_alignment.topic: Length, mass, capacity, temperature, money and time
source_alignment.source_statement: Choose and use appropriate standard units to estimate and measure length and height in any direction, mass, temperature and capacity to the nearest appropriate unit using rulers, scales, thermometers and measuring vessels; compare and order lengths, mass, volume/capacity and record the results using greater than, less than and equals; recognise and use symbols for pounds and pence, combine amounts to make a particular value, find different combinations of coins that equal the same amounts of money, and solve simple money problems; compare and sequence intervals of time, tell and write the time to five minutes, including quarter past/to the hour, and know the number of minutes in an hour and hours in a day.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack develops Year 2 measurement as practical decision making: choosing a suitable unit and tool, reading simple scales, comparing with symbols, making money amounts, and telling time to five minutes. It keeps estimation, recording and explanation central, and avoids trick scale-reading or adult shopping knowledge.

objective.child_goal: I can choose a measuring tool, read it carefully and explain what the measure means.
objective.statement: Estimate, measure, compare and solve simple problems involving length, mass, capacity, temperature, money and time using appropriate Year 2 units, tools and records.
misconceptions: Chooses an unsuitable unit or tool, such as measuring a pencil in litres or a jug with a ruler.
Starts reading a ruler, scale or number line from the wrong point instead of zero or the marked start.
Counts tick marks rather than intervals when reading a simple measuring scale.
Compares measures by the size of the object or container rather than the recorded unit value.
Assumes the largest number of coins is always worth the most money.
Confuses hour and minute hands or reads quarter to as quarter past.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A standard amount we use to measure, such as centimetre, metre, gram, kilogram, litre, millilitre, degree Celsius, pound, pence or minute.
vocabulary.audio_script: A unit tells us what kind of measure the number is counting.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Measure cards showing cm, m, g, kg, ml, l, degrees Celsius, p, pounds and min.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A sensible think-about-it guess before measuring.
vocabulary.audio_script: An estimate is a sensible guess. We can improve it by thinking about known measures.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A think bubble beside a ruler before the exact reading appears.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Marks on a tool that help us read a measure.
vocabulary.audio_script: A scale has marks. Read the spaces or labelled marks carefully.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A ruler, balance scale, measuring jug and thermometer with tick marks highlighted.

vocabulary.child_explanation: How much a container can hold.
vocabulary.audio_script: Capacity means how much fits inside a container.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A jug with liquid rising to a millilitre mark.

vocabulary.child_explanation: How much money is worth, not how many coins there are.
vocabulary.audio_script: Money value means what the coins are worth altogether.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Two coin sets with different coin counts but the same total value.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Quarter past is 15 minutes after the hour. Quarter to is 15 minutes before the next hour.
vocabulary.audio_script: Quarter past has the minute hand on three. Quarter to has the minute hand on nine.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Two analogue clocks with calm quarter segments shaded.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which known measure helps you estimate: a finger width, a metre stick, a full cup or one hour?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Activate benchmarks so estimates are reasoned rather than guesses.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Think of something you know. A centimetre is small, a metre is much longer, and one hour is sixty minutes.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Four benchmark cards appear beside a mystery measure, with audio labels and no scoring timer.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Choose the tool and unit for each job: length, mass, capacity, temperature, money or time.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Establish that useful measurement begins by matching the question to the right tool and unit.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Ask what you are measuring first. Then choose a tool that measures that thing.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A ruler, balance scale, measuring jug, thermometer, coin tray and clock are matched to labelled task cards.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Read the scale from zero. Count the intervals, not just the little lines.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model careful ruler and simple scale reading with attention to start point and interval size.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Start at zero. Each space is one centimetre. The pencil ends at twelve centimetres.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A pencil aligned to a ruler zero; later, a jug and thermometer show labelled intervals.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Measure two objects, then choose >, < or = and say the unit.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Link accurate measurement to comparison language and symbolic recording.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Read both measures with their units. Then choose the symbol that matches the comparison.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two measured objects or containers sit beside a comparison sentence with a unit badge.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Make the same amount of money in a different way, then build the clock time.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Check transfer to value equivalence and time to five minutes without relying on real-world shopping fluency.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Coins can look different and still have the same value. On a clock, the long minute hand tells the minutes.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A UK coin tray and an analogue clock face appear as separate low-clutter boards.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Pip measured from the end of the ruler, not zero. Teach Pip how to fix it.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Consolidate fair measurement, tool use and explanation through repair.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I put the pencil at the edge of the ruler. What should I line it up with?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A misaligned pencil is repaired by sliding its start to zero; the measure updates only after the repair.

manipulatives.purpose: Choose appropriate tools and standard units, then estimate, measure and compare length, mass, capacity and temperature.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Children select a measuring job, choose a tool and unit, then read a clear Year 2 scale by tapping the mark, using keyboard steps, switch scanning or an adult-recorded oral response.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Large marks, optional magnified scale, spoken unit labels, high-contrast tick patterns, no colour-only clues, no timed measuring, and reduced-motion static states support a range of access needs.

manipulatives.purpose: Build amounts in pounds and pence, compare values and find different coin combinations for the same amount.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners can drag coins, tap-to-add, use plus/minus steppers or choose from prepared coin sets; each coin has a spoken value and running total.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Coin size is not used as the only value cue; denominations are text-labelled and spoken, with large-print and symbol modes and an option to hide decorative coin detail.

manipulatives.purpose: Tell and make times to five minutes, including quarter past and quarter to, and compare simple time intervals.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Minute and hour hands can be moved separately or selected from labelled time cards; the clock narrates the hour hand and minute hand roles.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Static clock states, haptic-style tick descriptions, text equivalents, reduced hand overlap, replay and no requirement for analogue-only response support visual, motor and processing needs.

practice.interleaving_notes: Block tool-and-unit choice before scale reading. Interleave length, mass, capacity and temperature once start point and interval reading are stable; then mix comparison symbols, money equivalence, time to five minutes and short interval problems in calm spaced retrieval.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Choose appropriate tools and units for practical Year 2 measurement tasks and make sensible estimates.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: familiar measure task + 3 tool choices + 2-3 unit choices + benchmark estimate prompt
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep contexts culturally broad and school-safe; estimates should be plausible ranges, not exact hidden answers.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Read rulers, simple weighing scales, measuring vessels and thermometers to appropriate labelled or countable units.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: single uncluttered scale + zero/start cue + interval size + tap/read value + spoken unit
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Avoid ambiguous perspective, hidden starts and non-Year-2 increments; every answer must include the unit.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Compare and order measured quantities using >, < and = with consistent units.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: two or three measured objects + same-unit values + comparison symbol or order response + because sentence
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Do not compare unlike units without conversion; make visual appearance a plausible but not misleading distractor.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Recognise pounds and pence, combine coins to a target value and find different combinations for the same amount.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: target amount within Year 2 range + current UK coin set + build/select same value + running total
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use current UK denominations, avoid adult purchasing assumptions, and keep pounds/pence notation unambiguous.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Tell, make and sequence times to five minutes, including quarter past/to and simple intervals.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: spoken or written time + analogue clock state or builder + minute hand role cue + interval compare
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use accessible text equivalents and clear hour-hand placement; avoid contexts requiring knowledge of individual home routines.

body.prompt: You want to measure the length of a pencil. Which tool and unit would you choose?

interaction_route.touch: Tap one tool, one unit and one estimate card, then tap check.
interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the representation and choices; use arrow keys or plus/minus controls where offered, then Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, representation, choices or controls, check and retry in a fixed order; activate one item at a time.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select targets with adjustable dwell time and a confirm step for tools, scale values, symbols, coin sets and clock controls.
interaction_route.aac_or_adult_supported: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to select, enter or move the named response without the adult supplying the mathematical decision.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, pointing/AAC and adult-supported entry provide equivalent mathematical evidence; dragging, speech and handwriting are optional and never scored.
body.representation_access: Concrete-tool photographs, simple icons and a text table name each quantity, tool, unit and benchmark without colour-only meaning.
body.reduced_load_route: Show one tool/unit/estimate decision, scale interval, comparison, coin running total or clock hand at a time while keeping the unit and correct work visible.

hints: Ask what kind of measure you need: length, temperature or capacity.
A ruler measures length. A pencil is a small object, so centimetres are sensible.
question_variants.explanation: A ruler measures length, and centimetres are an appropriate unit for a pencil.

feedback.correct: You matched the measuring job to ruler and centimetres.
feedback.repair: Name the quantity first—length, mass, capacity or temperature—then match its tool, choose a suitably sized unit and compare the estimate with a familiar benchmark.
feedback.mathematical_evidence: ruler measures the requested quantity; centimetres records it appropriately.
feedback.misconception_support: unsuitable_unit_or_tool: Name the quantity first—length, mass, capacity or temperature—then match its tool, choose a suitably sized unit and compare the estimate with a familiar benchmark.
feedback.strategy_support: Use QUANTITY → TOOL → UNIT → BENCHMARK ESTIMATE.
feedback.support_message: Concrete, visual, text, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-supported routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct measure, unit, interval, coin total or clock-hand decision stays. Open one targeted representation and retry without losing progress.

body.prompt: The pencil starts at 0 cm and ends at 12 cm. How long is it?

interaction_route.touch: Tap a magnified mark or choose its labelled numeric value; precise pointer dragging is not required.
interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the representation and choices; use arrow keys or plus/minus controls where offered, then Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, representation, choices or controls, check and retry in a fixed order; activate one item at a time.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select targets with adjustable dwell time and a confirm step for tools, scale values, symbols, coin sets and clock controls.
interaction_route.aac_or_adult_supported: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to select, enter or move the named response without the adult supplying the mathematical decision.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, pointing/AAC and adult-supported entry provide equivalent mathematical evidence; dragging, speech and handwriting are optional and never scored.
body.representation_access: A magnified static scale, numbered tick/interval list and typed value alternative preserve start, interval and unit evidence.
body.reduced_load_route: Show one tool/unit/estimate decision, scale interval, comparison, coin running total or clock hand at a time while keeping the unit and correct work visible.

hints: Start reading from zero.
The end of the pencil lines up with the 12 cm mark.
question_variants.explanation: The pencil starts at 0 cm and reaches 12 cm, so it is 12 cm long.

feedback.correct: You read the marked scale as 12 cm by using its start and equal intervals.
feedback.repair: Locate zero or the stated start mark first, then count equal intervals from that point to the pointer; do not treat the edge of the picture as zero.
feedback.mathematical_evidence: The measure runs from 0 cm to 12 cm, giving 12 cm.
feedback.misconception_support: wrong_start_point: Locate zero or the stated start mark first, then count equal intervals from that point to the pointer; do not treat the edge of the picture as zero.
feedback.strategy_support: Use START → INTERVAL SIZE → COUNT SPACES → SAY VALUE WITH UNIT.
feedback.support_message: Concrete, visual, text, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-supported routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct measure, unit, interval, coin total or clock-hand decision stays. Open one targeted representation and retry without losing progress.

body.prompt: Jug A holds 600 ml. Jug B holds 450 ml. Choose the symbol that makes the sentence true.

interaction_route.touch: Tap >, < or = beside the two persistent same-unit records.
interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the representation and choices; use arrow keys or plus/minus controls where offered, then Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, representation, choices or controls, check and retry in a fixed order; activate one item at a time.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select targets with adjustable dwell time and a confirm step for tools, scale values, symbols, coin sets and clock controls.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, pointing/AAC and adult-supported entry provide equivalent mathematical evidence; dragging, speech and handwriting are optional and never scored.
body.representation_access: Recorded values remain visible with symbol words greater than, less than and equal to available beside >, < and =.
body.reduced_load_route: Show one tool/unit/estimate decision, scale interval, comparison, coin running total or clock hand at a time while keeping the unit and correct work visible.

hints: Both measures are in millilitres, so compare the numbers.
Six hundred is greater than four hundred and fifty.
question_variants.explanation: Six hundred millilitres is greater than four hundred and fifty millilitres, so 600 ml > 450 ml.

feedback.correct: You compared measures in the same unit and chose >, so 600 ml > 450 ml.
feedback.repair: Keep both unit labels visible, compare the recorded numbers in the same unit and then select >, < or =; object or container appearance is not the measure.
feedback.mathematical_evidence: 600 ml > 450 ml; both records use the same shown unit.
feedback.misconception_support: appearance_over_recorded_measure: Keep both unit labels visible, compare the recorded numbers in the same unit and then select >, < or =; object or container appearance is not the measure.
feedback.strategy_support: Use SAME UNIT → COMPARE VALUES → READ THE SYMBOL SENTENCE.
feedback.support_message: Concrete, visual, text, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-supported routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct measure, unit, interval, coin total or clock-hand decision stays. Open one targeted representation and retry without losing progress.

interaction_route.touch: Tap a prepared coin set or use labelled add/remove steppers and a running total; coin dragging is optional.
interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the representation and choices; use arrow keys or plus/minus controls where offered, then Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, representation, choices or controls, check and retry in a fixed order; activate one item at a time.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select targets with adjustable dwell time and a confirm step for tools, scale values, symbols, coin sets and clock controls.

body.representation_access: UK denominations have large text labels, optional simplified outlines and a running pence total; real-coin handling is not required.
body.reduced_load_route: Show one tool/unit/estimate decision, scale interval, comparison, coin running total or clock hand at a time while keeping the unit and correct work visible.

hints: Add the value of the coins, not the number of coins.
question_variants.explanation: The set with 20p, 20p and 10p has a total value of 50p.

feedback.correct: You added coin values—not coin count—and made 50p exactly with 20p + 20p + 10p.
feedback.repair: Read each denomination, add a running value total and stop only when it equals the target; more coins do not necessarily mean more money.
feedback.mathematical_evidence: 20p + 20p + 10p = 50p; the denomination sum, not the number of coins, is the evidence.
feedback.misconception_support: coin_count_over_value: Read each denomination, add a running value total and stop only when it equals the target; more coins do not necessarily mean more money.
feedback.strategy_support: Use READ DENOMINATIONS → RUNNING TOTAL → EXACT TARGET CHECK.
feedback.support_message: Concrete, visual, text, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-supported routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct measure, unit, interval, coin total or clock-hand decision stays. Open one targeted representation and retry without losing progress.

body.prompt: Build quarter to 3 on the clock.
choices: minute hand on 9 and hour hand nearly at 3
minute hand on 3 and hour hand just after 3
minute hand on 12 and hour hand on 3

interaction_route.touch: Tap a prepared clock state or use separate labelled five-minute and hour-hand steppers; hand dragging is optional.
interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the representation and choices; use arrow keys or plus/minus controls where offered, then Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, representation, choices or controls, check and retry in a fixed order; activate one item at a time.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select targets with adjustable dwell time and a confirm step for tools, scale values, symbols, coin sets and clock controls.

body.representation_access: Static high-contrast clock, separate hour/minute controls, five-minute number track and full text/digital equivalent avoid analogue-only access.
body.reduced_load_route: Show one tool/unit/estimate decision, scale interval, comparison, coin running total or clock hand at a time while keeping the unit and correct work visible.

expected_answer.value: minute hand on 9 and hour hand nearly at 3

hints: Quarter to means 15 minutes before the next hour.
The long minute hand points to 9.
question_variants.explanation: Quarter to 3 is 15 minutes before 3 o'clock, so the minute hand is on 9 and the hour hand is nearly at 3.

feedback.correct: You coordinated the minute and hour hands to show 2:45: minute hand on 9 and hour hand nearly at 3.
feedback.repair: Mark 15 minutes: minute hand on 3 means quarter past; minute hand on 9 means quarter to the next hour. Then place the hour hand between the correct hours.
feedback.mathematical_evidence: 2:45 is represented by minute hand on 9 and hour hand nearly at 3; the long hand gives minutes and the short hand shows the hour position.
feedback.misconception_support: quarter_to_quarter_past_confusion: Mark 15 minutes: minute hand on 3 means quarter past; minute hand on 9 means quarter to the next hour. Then place the hour hand between the correct hours.
feedback.strategy_support: Use MINUTE HAND IN FIVES → HOUR HAND POSITION → DIGITAL/TEXT CHECK.
feedback.support_message: Concrete, visual, text, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-supported routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct measure, unit, interval, coin total or clock-hand decision stays. Open one targeted representation and retry without losing progress.

feedback.repair: Name the quantity first—length, mass, capacity or temperature—then match its tool, choose a suitably sized unit and compare the estimate with a familiar benchmark.
feedback.mathematical_evidence: thermometer measures the requested quantity; degrees Celsius records it appropriately and 30 degrees Celsius is the selected benchmark-sized estimate.
feedback.misconception_support: unsuitable_unit_or_tool: Name the quantity first—length, mass, capacity or temperature—then match its tool, choose a suitably sized unit and compare the estimate with a familiar benchmark.
feedback.strategy_support: Use QUANTITY → TOOL → UNIT → BENCHMARK ESTIMATE.
feedback.support_message: Concrete, visual, text, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-supported routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct measure, unit, interval, coin total or clock-hand decision stays. Open one targeted representation and retry without losing progress.

body.prompt: At the measure station, help Bo choose a tool, unit and sensible estimate for the temperature of icy water.
body.measuring_job: the temperature of icy water

interaction_route.touch: Tap one tool, one unit and one estimate card, then tap check.
interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the representation and choices; use arrow keys or plus/minus controls where offered, then Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, representation, choices or controls, check and retry in a fixed order; activate one item at a time.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select targets with adjustable dwell time and a confirm step for tools, scale values, symbols, coin sets and clock controls.
interaction_route.aac_or_adult_supported: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to select, enter or move the named response without the adult supplying the mathematical decision.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, pointing/AAC and adult-supported entry provide equivalent mathematical evidence; dragging, speech and handwriting are optional and never scored.
body.representation_access: Concrete-tool photographs, simple icons and a text table name each quantity, tool, unit and benchmark without colour-only meaning.
body.reduced_load_route: Show one tool/unit/estimate decision, scale interval, comparison, coin running total or clock hand at a time while keeping the unit and correct work visible.

hints: Find the labelled start before counting.
Each space changes by 2 degrees Celsius.
question_variants.explanation: Starting at -10 degrees Celsius and counting 11 equal intervals gives 12 degrees Celsius.

feedback.correct: You read the marked scale as 12 degrees Celsius by using its start and equal intervals.
feedback.repair: Locate zero or the stated start mark first, then count equal intervals from that point to the pointer; do not treat the edge of the picture as zero.
feedback.mathematical_evidence: -10 + 11 × 2 = 12 degrees Celsius.

feedback.strategy_support: Use START → INTERVAL SIZE → COUNT SPACES → SAY VALUE WITH UNIT.
feedback.support_message: Concrete, visual, text, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-supported routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.

interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the representation and choices; use arrow keys or plus/minus controls where offered, then Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, representation, choices or controls, check and retry in a fixed order; activate one item at a time.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select targets with adjustable dwell time and a confirm step for tools, scale values, symbols, coin sets and clock controls.
interaction_route.aac_or_adult_supported: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to select, enter or move the named response without the adult supplying the mathematical decision.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, pointing/AAC and adult-supported entry provide equivalent mathematical evidence; dragging, speech and handwriting are optional and never scored.
body.representation_access: A magnified static scale, numbered tick/interval list and typed value alternative preserve start, interval and unit evidence.
body.reduced_load_route: Show one tool/unit/estimate decision, scale interval, comparison, coin running total or clock hand at a time while keeping the unit and correct work visible.

hints: Find the labelled start before counting.
Each space changes by 50 g.
question_variants.explanation: Starting at 0 g and counting 15 equal intervals gives 750 g.

feedback.correct: You read the marked scale as 750 g by using its start and equal intervals.
feedback.repair: Locate zero or the stated start mark first, then count equal intervals from that point to the pointer; do not treat the edge of the picture as zero.
feedback.misconception_support: wrong_start_point: Locate zero or the stated start mark first, then count equal intervals from that point to the pointer; do not treat the edge of the picture as zero.
feedback.strategy_support: Use START → INTERVAL SIZE → COUNT SPACES → SAY VALUE WITH UNIT.
feedback.support_message: Concrete, visual, text, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-supported routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct measure, unit, interval, coin total or clock-hand decision stays. Open one targeted representation and retry without losing progress.

body.prompt: Compare 12 cm and 12 cm. Which symbol makes the statement true?

interaction_route.touch: Tap >, < or = beside the two persistent same-unit records.
interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the representation and choices; use arrow keys or plus/minus controls where offered, then Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, representation, choices or controls, check and retry in a fixed order; activate one item at a time.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select targets with adjustable dwell time and a confirm step for tools, scale values, symbols, coin sets and clock controls.
interaction_route.aac_or_adult_supported: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to select, enter or move the named response without the adult supplying the mathematical decision.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, pointing/AAC and adult-supported entry provide equivalent mathematical evidence; dragging, speech and handwriting are optional and never scored.
body.representation_access: Recorded values remain visible with symbol words greater than, less than and equal to available beside >, < and =.
body.reduced_load_route: Show one tool/unit/estimate decision, scale interval, comparison, coin running total or clock hand at a time while keeping the unit and correct work visible.

hints: Check that both records use the same unit.
Compare the number values, then say the symbol aloud.
question_variants.explanation: 12 cm = 12 cm.

feedback.correct: You compared measures in the same unit and chose =, so 12 cm = 12 cm.
feedback.repair: Keep both unit labels visible, compare the recorded numbers in the same unit and then select >, < or =; object or container appearance is not the measure.
feedback.mathematical_evidence: 12 cm = 12 cm; both records use cm.
feedback.misconception_support: appearance_over_recorded_measure: Keep both unit labels visible, compare the recorded numbers in the same unit and then select >, < or =; object or container appearance is not the measure.
feedback.strategy_support: Use SAME UNIT → COMPARE VALUES → READ THE SYMBOL SENTENCE.
feedback.support_message: Concrete, visual, text, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-supported routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct measure, unit, interval, coin total or clock-hand decision stays. Open one targeted representation and retry without losing progress.

choices: minute hand on 5 and hour hand just after 1

interaction_route.touch: Tap a prepared clock state or use separate labelled five-minute and hour-hand steppers; hand dragging is optional.
interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the representation and choices; use arrow keys or plus/minus controls where offered, then Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, representation, choices or controls, check and retry in a fixed order; activate one item at a time.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select targets with adjustable dwell time and a confirm step for tools, scale values, symbols, coin sets and clock controls.

body.representation_access: Static high-contrast clock, separate hour/minute controls, five-minute number track and full text/digital equivalent avoid analogue-only access.
body.reduced_load_route: Show one tool/unit/estimate decision, scale interval, comparison, coin running total or clock hand at a time while keeping the unit and correct work visible.

expected_answer.value: minute hand on 5 and hour hand just after 1

hints: Count around the clock in five-minute steps for the long hand.
Then place the short hand to show the hour it has passed or is moving towards.
question_variants.explanation: 25 minutes past 1 is 1:25; minute hand on 5 and hour hand just after 1.

feedback.correct: You coordinated the minute and hour hands to show 1:25: minute hand on 5 and hour hand just after 1.
feedback.repair: Separate the hands: count the long minute hand in five-minute steps first, then place the short hour hand on, just after, halfway between or nearly at the correct hour.
feedback.mathematical_evidence: 1:25 is represented by minute hand on 5 and hour hand just after 1; the long hand gives minutes and the short hand shows the hour position.
feedback.misconception_support: hour_minute_hand_confusion: Separate the hands: count the long minute hand in five-minute steps first, then place the short hour hand on, just after, halfway between or nearly at the correct hour.
feedback.strategy_support: Use MINUTE HAND IN FIVES → HOUR HAND POSITION → DIGITAL/TEXT CHECK.
feedback.support_message: Concrete, visual, text, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-supported routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct measure, unit, interval, coin total or clock-hand decision stays. Open one targeted representation and retry without losing progress.

feedback.repair: Separate the hands: count the long minute hand in five-minute steps first, then place the short hour hand on, just after, halfway between or nearly at the correct hour.
feedback.mathematical_evidence: 2:40 is represented by minute hand on 8 and hour hand nearly at 3; the long hand gives minutes and the short hand shows the hour position.
feedback.misconception_support: hour_minute_hand_confusion: Separate the hands: count the long minute hand in five-minute steps first, then place the short hour hand on, just after, halfway between or nearly at the correct hour.
feedback.strategy_support: Use MINUTE HAND IN FIVES → HOUR HAND POSITION → DIGITAL/TEXT CHECK.
feedback.support_message: Concrete, visual, text, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-supported routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct measure, unit, interval, coin total or clock-hand decision stays. Open one targeted representation and retry without losing progress.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Chooses an unsuitable unit or tool, such as measuring a pencil in litres or a jug with a ruler.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner selects a tool or unit that measures a different attribute from the task.
repair_sequence: Name the attribute first: length, mass, capacity, temperature, money or time.
Match the attribute to a tool picture and a spoken unit.
Measure or solve a fresh example with the tool and unit visible.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner chooses a suitable tool and unit for a new practical measuring job.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Starts reading a ruler, scale or number line from the wrong point instead of zero or the marked start.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner counts from the physical edge of the tool or from 1 instead of the start mark.
repair_sequence: Highlight the start mark and place the object start there.
Cover the irrelevant edge of the tool.
Read the end mark and say the unit.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner reads a new aligned ruler or scale item from the correct start point.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Counts tick marks rather than intervals when reading a simple measuring scale.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner gives an answer one interval too high or treats unequal tick spacing as equal.
repair_sequence: Highlight one space between marks as one interval.
Count jumps from zero to the pointer or liquid level.
Replay with labelled marks visible, then fade the labels.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner reads a new simple scale and explains the interval size.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Compares measures by the size of the object or container rather than the recorded unit value.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner chooses the taller, wider or more decorative object despite a smaller measured value.
repair_sequence: Hide decorative details and keep only the measured records.
Check both values have the same unit.
Choose >, < or = and complete a spoken because sentence.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner compares a fresh pair using recorded values and units.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Assumes the largest number of coins is always worth the most money.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner chooses many low-value coins over fewer higher-value coins without adding values.
repair_sequence: Add coin values into a running total.
Compare two sets with different coin counts but the same or different values.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner identifies a same-value coin combination and explains with total value.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Confuses hour and minute hands or reads quarter to as quarter past.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner swaps hand roles or places the minute hand on 3 for quarter to.
repair_sequence: Label the long minute hand and short hour hand.
Shade the quarter segment before or after the hour.
Build quarter past and quarter to as a contrast pair.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner builds a new quarter past/to time and describes both hand positions.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use plain measuring boards, quiet confirmation sounds, no pouring splashes, no ticking clock audio and one tool at a time.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace liquid fills, pointer sweeps, coin movement and clock-hand rotations with static before-and-after states and labelled stages.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read tools, units, scale values, coin values, comparison symbols and clock times aloud with replay.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use short prompts, consistent unit badges, symbol narration and picture cues; written notation is paired with audio and objects.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Use a fixed choose-tool, estimate, measure, record, check routine with unused controls hidden.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow magnified scales, stepwise mark counting, auto-align, undo, reduced choices and unlimited thinking time.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Separate motor placement from mathematical judgement, preserve correct selections during repair and never score speed.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach length, height, mass, capacity, temperature, value, estimate, unit, scale, greater than, less than, quarter past and quarter to with objects and gestures.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires suitable tool and unit choice, accurate reading of clear Year 2 scales, comparison using units and symbols, and supported money/time tasks. Secure requires retained scale reading, money equivalence and time-to-five-minute reasoning across mixed contexts after spaced review.
evidence.parent_summary: Can choose measuring tools and units, read simple scales, compare measures, make money amounts and tell times to five minutes.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Records whether the learner chose the attribute/tool/unit, read from the correct start point, counted scale intervals, compared with units, added money values and placed clock hands accurately.
next_actions: Revisit unstable scale reading with zero-start and interval-highlight repair.
Use practical classroom measuring before abstract comparison-only items.
Continue money value work with running totals, not coin-count shortcuts.
Schedule clock retrieval using contrast pairs such as quarter past and quarter to.

qa.notes: Quality-hardened deterministic 260-item Year 2 measurement pilot. IDs, answers, five curated cores, blueprint allocation, representations, arithmetic and curriculum scope remain unchanged. Every variant now has concept-specific correct feedback, misconception repair, mathematical evidence and strategy support, plus explicit touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC/adult-supported and no-drag response routes. Speech and handwriting are optional, retry is pressure-free, and dyscalculia supports preserve units, benchmarks, scale intervals, running totals and static clock/text equivalents. Variant narration remains selectively absent; any future narration must use produced, human-reviewed ElevenLabs assets and browser TTS is prohibited. Independent mathematics, SEND, renderer and classroom review remain required before promotion.

accessibility_policy.no_timer_or_penalty: Learners are never timed for mastery and never lose earned progress for mistakes; incorrect attempts route to calm repair, replay and retry.
accessibility_policy.equivalent_response_routes: Every child-facing task must preserve an equivalent route through touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing, partner-mediated or adult-scribed response where appropriate.
accessibility_policy.audio_equivalent: Audio and read-aloud support must complement visible text, visuals and replay. Child-facing narration must use produced, human-listening-approved assets before release; browser TTS is not a teaching or phonics fallback.
accessibility_policy.reduced_motion: Reduced-motion and static alternatives must preserve the same curriculum evidence, feedback and reward meaning without relying on animation.
